title: Quantification of SARS-CoV-2 Using Nanotrap Particles and Digital PCR System
author: Solana Narum
materials: Qiagen AllPrep PowerViral DNA/RNA Extraction Kit
Thaw GT-Molecular controls and assay solutions on ice. If necessary, also thaw the extracted RNA on ice. Once thawed, vortex to mix.
Warm PM1 from the Qiagen AllPrep PowerViral DNA/RNA Extraction Kit Qiagen Catalog #28000-50 at 55 °C for 5-10 minutes before use.
In the dPCR Parameters section under the Priming tab, select the Qiagen Standard Priming Profile.
To each tube add 6 µL 2-mercaptoethanol and 600 µL PM1 from the Qiagen AllPrep PowerViral DNA/RNA Kit and invert to mix.

steps.title: Composite primary influent samples are collected...
steps.instruction: Composite primary influent samples are collected (grab samples, flow composite, or time composite)

steps.title: 50 mL sub-samples are collected in...
steps.instruction: 50 mL sub-samples are collected in 50 mL sterile conical tubes. The samples are stored at 4 °C until further processing.

steps.title: These steps should be performed in...
steps.instruction: These steps should be performed in a Class II Biosafety Cabinet, which has been cleaned with RNase Away and water.

steps.title: Thaw an aliquot of BCoV working...
steps.instruction: Thaw an aliquot of BCoV working ( Bovilis Coronavirus Calf Vaccine Merck Animal Health Catalog #16445 ) On ice (see section Preparation of Bovilis Coronavirus). Pipette up and down to mix the aliquot and briefly centrifuge.

steps.title: Add 62 µL of BCoV working...
steps.instruction: Add 62 µL of BCoV working to each 50 mL wastewater sample and invert to mix.

steps.title: Using a serological pipette transfer 10...
steps.instruction: Using a serological pipette transfer 10 mL of the wastewater sample into a 15 mL conical tube. Repeat this for a duplicate.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Nanotrap Enhancement...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Nanotrap Enhancement Reagent 2 to each 10 mL wastewater sample and vortex for a few seconds.

steps.title: Add 150 µL of Nanotrap Magnetic...
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL of Nanotrap Magnetic Virus Particles and invert 2-3 times to mix.

steps.title: Incubate the samples for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples for 10 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Place the sample tubes in a 15 mL magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Place the sample tubes in a 15 mL magnetic rack. Wait 1 minute. Remove the supernatant using a serological pipette. The magnetic particles form a pellet on the sides of the tube so it is essential to hold the tip of the pipette in the center of the tube while removing the supernatant. Transfer the supernatant to a waste container.

steps.title: Proper aseptic technique should be used when working with RNA
steps.instruction: Proper aseptic technique should be used when working with RNA. Always wear powder-free latex, vinyl, or nitrile gloves while handling reagents, tubes, and RNA samples to prevent RNase contamination from the surface of the skin or from the environment. Change gloves frequently and keep tubes closed. During the procedure work quickly and keep everything on cold blocks when possible, to avoid degradation of RNA by endogenous or residual RNAses. Clean working surfaces and pipettes with RNase away. The first step should be performed in the Class II Biosafety Cabinet decontaminated with UV light and RNase away. After the lysis solutions have been applied in step 9, the additional steps can take place outside of the Biosafety Cabinet.

steps.title: Incubate the samples including the extraction...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples including the extraction control for 10 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Place the 1
steps.instruction: Place the 1.5 mL sample tubes on the magnetic rack and wait 1 minute before transferring the supernatant into the center tube of the rotor adapter.

steps.title: Place the QIAcube rotor adapters in...
steps.instruction: Place the QIAcube rotor adapters in the QIAcube centrifuge and follow the instructions on the QIAcube control tablet to set up the shaker rack, reagents, and tips. When setting up the reagents, shake to mix the PM5 buffer. Set the elution volume to 100 µL . Start the extraction run. QIAcube Connect (Automated nucleic acid extraction, Qiagen, 9002864, https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/discovery-and-translational-research/dna-rna-purification/instruments-equipment/qiacube-connect/ )

steps.title: When the extraction is completed, cap...
steps.instruction: When the extraction is completed, cap the elution tubes and begin the dPCR steps or store at -80 °C if the dPCR run will occur in the following days.

steps.title: Discard the used pipette tips and...
steps.instruction: Discard the used pipette tips and wipe the waste drawer and QIAcube workspace with 70% ethanol. After each run, remove the plastic tube holder and the reagent tray before running 2 cycles of UV decontamination.

steps.title: Dilute 1 µL extracted RNA with...
steps.instruction: Dilute 1 µL extracted RNA with 99 µL RNase-free water for PMMoV analysis.

steps.title: Prepare master mixes for PMMoV and N1-N2-BCoV assays
steps.instruction: Prepare master mixes for PMMoV and N1-N2-BCoV assays. Allow for one extra sample. Vortex to mix. Briefly centrifuge the tubes to collect the master mix at the bottom of the tube. %s %s %s %s GT-Digital SARS-CoV-2 Wastewater Surveillance Assay for QIAcuity GT Molecular Qiagen QIAcuity One-Step Viral RT-PCR Kit Qiagen Catalog #1123145

steps.title: Pipette 20 µL of the appropriate...
steps.instruction: Pipette 20 µL of the appropriate master mix (N1-N2-BCoV or PMMoV) into the wells of a PCR strip tube.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of extracted RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of extracted RNA sample or positive control to the PCR strip tube following the planned layout. Use the 1:100 diluted samples for the wells being used for the PMMoV assay. After transferring, pipette gently to mix. Keep the PCR strips on ice while loading.

steps.title: For the non-template control
steps.instruction: For the non-template control: pipette 20 µL of molecular grade water into a PCR tube in place of adding extracted RNA.

steps.title: Place a Qiagen QIAcuity 26k 24-well...
steps.instruction: Place a Qiagen QIAcuity 26k 24-well Nanoplate onto the Nanoplate protection tray. If the tray is not used, dust can collect on the bottom side of the plate and interfere with the imaging step. Occasionally wipe the tray with 70% ethanol to clean dust.

steps.title: Using a multichannel pipette, transfer 39...
steps.instruction: Using a multichannel pipette, transfer 39 µL of solution from the PCR strips to their respective location on the Nanoplate. Be careful to not transfer air bubbles during this step. Do not push the pipette beyond the first stop during this step.

steps.title: Carefully seal the Nanoplate with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully seal the Nanoplate with a Nanoplate seal and the roller provided with the QIAcuity Instrument.

steps.title: Place the sealed plate in the...
steps.instruction: Place the sealed plate in the plate drawer inside the QIAcuity instrument. QIAcuity One, 5-plex (dPCR, Qiagen, 911021, https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/instruments-and-automation/pcr-instruments/qiacuity-digital-pcr-system/?catno=911021 )

steps.title: Setup the plate by selecting "New Plate"
steps.instruction: Setup the plate by selecting "New Plate". Name the plate and choose the plate type "Nanoplate 26k 24-well".

steps.title: Under the Cycling tab create the cycling conditions shown below
steps.instruction: Under the Cycling tab create the cycling conditions shown below. These are the conditions recommended by the GT Molecular Wastewater Surveillance Guide.

steps.title: In the Imaging tab create the conditions shown below
steps.instruction: In the Imaging tab create the conditions shown below. These settings are different from the GT Molecular recommended settings, but provides less frequent saturation errors.

steps.title: Navigate to "Reaction mixes"
steps.instruction: Navigate to "Reaction mixes". Create reaction mixes named "N1-N2-BCoV Triplex Assay" and "PMMoV Assay" that contain the following details.

steps.title: Navigate to "Samples and controls"
steps.instruction: Navigate to "Samples and controls". Add samples names that are being quantified on this run. Extraction controls should be added as samples. Under the "Controls" tab create both a "N1-N2-BCoV Positive Control" and a "PMMoV Positive Control". Under the Non Template Controls" tab create a "N1-N2-BCoV dPCRNeg" and a "PMMoV dPCRNeg".

steps.title: Navigate to "Plate Layout"
steps.instruction: Navigate to "Plate Layout". Assign reaction mixes, samples and controls to their wells. Save plate and exit the setup.

steps.title: On the QIAcuity tablet, select the...
steps.instruction: On the QIAcuity tablet, select the plate and run the reaction.

steps.title: When the QIAcuity run is complete,...
steps.instruction: When the QIAcuity run is complete, ensure the image transfer is marked as complete in the QIAcuity Software Suite before inspecting the data.

steps.title: Open the plate results by selecting "Analysis"
steps.instruction: Open the plate results by selecting "Analysis". Select all the wells and targets before selecting "Show results".

steps.title: In the 1D Scatterplot tab verify...
steps.instruction: In the 1D Scatterplot tab verify that the automatic threshold is accurately placed between the negative and positive partitions. If needed, adjust the threshold placement to the accurate position.

steps.title: Use the "Export to CSV" button...
steps.instruction: Use the "Export to CSV" button in the List tab to export the data.

steps.title: The Bovilis Coronavirus (BCoV) is used...
steps.instruction: The Bovilis Coronavirus (BCoV) is used as internal process control and is added to each sample prior to filtration and extraction. It is necessary to quantify BCoV for each new batch of BCoV working is made from a new tube of BCoV ND . BCoV should not be vortexed at any point in the preparation or spiking of samples.

steps.title: Bovilis Coronavirus Calf Vaccine Merck Animal...
steps.instruction: Bovilis Coronavirus Calf Vaccine Merck Animal Health Catalog #16445 is lyophilized when received. Reconstitute the virus in 5 mL pre-chilled molecular grade water and swirl to mix.

steps.title: Aliquot 100 µL stock in sterile 1
steps.instruction: Aliquot 100 µL stock in sterile 1.5 mL tubes and label each tube BCoV ND (non-diluted). Store BCoV ND at -80 °C

steps.title: Dilute 60 µL BCoV ND with...
steps.instruction: Dilute 60 µL BCoV ND with 540 µL pre-chilled molecular grade water and pipette to mix. Briefly centrifuge. Label this tube BCoV INT (intermediate).

steps.title: Dilute 500 µL BCoV INT with 49
steps.instruction: Dilute 500 µL BCoV INT with 49.5 mL pre-chilled molecular grade water and invert to mix. Aliquot 1 mL of BCoV working into sterile 1.5 mL tubes and store aliquots at -80 °C .

steps.title: To quantify the BCoV spike, extract...
steps.instruction: To quantify the BCoV spike, extract the RNA of an aliquot of BCoV working in triplicate.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of BCoV working working into three 1
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of BCoV working working into three 1.5 mL tubes.

steps.title: Microcentrifuge the tubes at 13000 x...
steps.instruction: Microcentrifuge the tubes at 13000 x g, °C, for 1 minute.

steps.title: Add the supernatant to the center...
steps.instruction: Add the supernatant to the center column of a rotor adapter and continue the extraction in the Qiagen QIAcube.

steps.title: Quantify the extracted BCoV working RNA...
steps.instruction: Quantify the extracted BCoV working RNA by analyzing the extraction triplicates using the same dPCR steps beginning in the Detection and Quantification section .

steps.title: To calculate BCoV working concentration, use...
steps.instruction: To calculate BCoV working concentration, use the average measured concentration in copies/microL of the 3 replicates analyzed by dPCR.

steps.title: Add 1 mL molecular grade water...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL molecular grade water to each 15 mL tube and remove from magnetic rack. Invert the sample to collect the Nanotrap Magnetic Particles in the water.

steps.title: Transfer the sample using a pipet to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample using a pipet to a 1.5 mL tube. Place the 1.5 mL tubes on a 1.5 mL magnetic rack for 1 minute to allow the magnetic particles to separate from the supernatant. Discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 200 µL Zymo DNA/RNA Shield...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL Zymo DNA/RNA Shield Fisher Scientific Catalog #50-125-1706 to each sample and vortex to mix. If extraction is delayed, store samples up to 3 days at 4 °C .

steps.title: Add 6 µL 2-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma...
steps.instruction: Add 6 µL 2-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M6250 and 600 µL PM1 to each 1.5 mL tube containing the concentrated sample and Nanotrap Magnetic Virus Particles. Vortex to mix.

steps.title: Prepare an extraction control by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare an extraction control by adding 6 µL 2-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M6250 , 600 µL PM1, and 200 µL RNA Shield to an empty tube. Also add 100 µL Nanotrap Enhancement Reagent 2 and 150 µL Nanotrap Magnetic Virus Particles.

steps.title: The QIAcuity instrument may show an...
steps.instruction: The QIAcuity instrument may show an error when viewing results that a "channel has reached saturation". To resolve this error, reduce the imaging gain and exposure duration and re-image the plate.

description: Technical notes: The dPCR instrument is sensitive to microbubbles. To prevent errors in quantification and imaging, ensure that pipettes only reach the first stop when preparing this reaction. The preparation of the dPCR plate should be done in a PCR workstation previously decontaminated by UV light and has been cleaned with RNase away and water.
